summary.intent: Propagate the `verbose` flag through the DocsFinder summarization call chain so that provider-level logs appear during DocsFinder summarization when verbose mode is enabled.
summary.outcome: Added `verbose?: boolean` to `summarizeDocFile` and threaded it through to `invokeIsolated` in `docs-finder.ts`. All docs-finder tests pass, lint is clean, and the fix was committed.

repo: The top-level `bun run test` command picks up `tmp/clones/` (externally cloned repos), producing ~114 spurious failures; use `bun test src/` to run only project tests.
114 pre-existing test failures exist in the suite — a mix of intentional error-throwing tests and external clone artifacts — and are not regressions.
`invokeIsolated` in `src/shared/utils/isolated-prompt.ts` already forwards all options (including `verbose`) to `provider.invoke`; no changes were needed there.
Git operations are performed via an `rtk` wrapper prefix rather than plain `git`.

code.finding: `summarizeDocFile` signature — added optional `verbose?: boolean` parameter and passed it to `invokeIsolated`.
`summarizeLink` closure inside `discoverLinkedDocs` — added `verbose` argument to the `summarizeDocFile` call.

workflow: When a global test run produces unexpected failures, scope the run to `src/` before investigating; external artifacts in `tmp/` can pollute results.
Filter test output with `grep -E` patterns targeting pass/fail counts to quickly confirm project-level test health.

friction: `bun run test` surfaced 114 failures from `tmp/clones/`, requiring multiple follow-up commands to confirm that only the pre-existing failures were present and docs-finder tests were clean.
Had to run several incremental `bun test` + `grep` invocations to isolate the relevant pass/fail signal.
open_items: `tmp/clones/` is not excluded from the test runner glob; adding it to the test exclusion config would prevent future confusion.
The 114 pre-existing test failures (intentional error-throwing tests + external clone artifacts) were left unaddressed — worth auditing to determine which are truly intentional vs. stale noise.
